title: Leader-Follower Strategy Style Priors
summary: Public motorsport strategy work describes pit decisions as adversarial, with leader-follower behavior and undercut avoidance materially affecting outcomes.
rationale: Public motorsport strategy work describes pit decisions as adversarial, with leader-follower behavior and undercut avoidance materially affecting outcomes. Two drivers with similar tyre state can still differ because one historically pits proactively while another reacts.
implementation_hint: Create OOF priors for each driver/team on proactive vs reactive behavior: probability of being first among local peers to pit, average deviation from field-median stop lap, tendency to extend after a rival pits, and tendency to defend track position when within pit window. Shrink to team/year/race-type priors for sparse or unseen drivers.
expected_effect: Separates otherwise similar rows when entity strategy tendencies matter more than tyre age alone.
risk: Can overfit historical dominance or driver identity if not strongly regularized; unseen-driver handling must fall back cleanly.
